mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising faster than FEDFUNDS, markets infer policy will stay restrictive or tighten further, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days through higher expected short rates and term premium.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation momentum exceeds FEDFUNDS by a widening margin
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day Treasury yield changes are not positive in most episodes where CPIAUCSL acceleration exceeds FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips negative after the trigger repeatedly over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS alongside a rise in UNRATE signals labor-market softening and weaker growth expectations, which lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days via reduced real-rate and inflation expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.2pp over 2 months while PAYEMS declines
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day yield changes more often than negative ones, or if the relationship disappears after controlling for contemporaneous CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS indicates resilient demand and firmer nominal activity, which lifts inflation and growth expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if rolling 3-year Rank IC on 20-day yield changes is below 0.03 or if strong RSAFS growth is followed by negative yield changes more often than positive ones.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO from a weak base signals firmer industrial activity and capacity use, which raises cyclical growth expectations and tends to lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month after at least 2 prior months of weakness
failure_condition: Reject if post-rebound episodes do not show positive average 20-day yield changes, or if the sign is negative in a majority of rebound instances.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in HOUST after prior weakness reflects stronger housing demand and easier financial conditions, which raises near-term growth and inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases more than 5% month-over-month after a prior decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-surprise episodes are followed by flat or negative 20-day yield changes more often than positive ones, or if the effect vanishes once FEDFUNDS is stable.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL cools while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, the market prices future cuts and lower term premium, which pulls the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year decelerates for 2 consecutive monthly prints while FEDFUNDS stays unchanged or higher
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation-plus-high-rates episodes do not produce negative yield changes in at least 60% of cases, or if yields rise despite sustained CPIAUCSL cooling.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE indicates broad labor tightening and stronger wage pressure expectations, which lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next quarter by increasing expected inflation and policy persistence.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-strength signal fails to produce positive 60-day yield changes in most instances, or if the effect becomes negative when inflation is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 combined with weaker RSAFS signals demand compression and falling nominal activity, which lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations reset lower.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth decelerates while RSAFS growth is negative on a monthly basis
failure_condition: Reject if demand-slowdown episodes do not yield negative 60-day Treasury moves, or if positive yield changes dominate despite concurrent GDPC1 and RSAFS weakening.
